definition:
1. to drive  or thrust through  a thing, with genitive, (Odyssey by Homer); also with double accusative, λόγχην δ. στέρνα (Euripides) 
2. to let  people go through  a country, give  them a passage through , (Xenophon Historicus):—; with genitive, ξυμφορὰς τοῦ σοῦ διῆκας στόματος didst let  them pass through  thy mouth, gavest utterance to  them, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
3. to send apart, to dismiss, disband , (Xenophon Historicus) 
4. to dissolve : in Mid., διέμενος ὄξει having diluted  it with vinegar, (Aristophanes Comicus) (ML)
